commit.message:
fix: replace corrupted vendor.js with correct copy from distro

The vendor.js was double-escaped and truncated to 36% of its correct
size (21KB vs 58KB). All backslash-quote sequences were double-escaped
causing "Invalid or unexpected token" at line 13, and Preact Hooks,
htm, and marked.js sections were entirely missing.

Replaced with byte-for-byte copy from amplifier-distro source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use unique coverage filenames per test project (#32)

## Problem

All test projects were writing their coverage output to the same file
(`coverage.cobertura.xml`), causing each project to overwrite the
previous one's report.

## Solution

- Removed `--coverage-output coverage.cobertura.xml` from the `dotnet
test` command so that the test runner auto-generates a unique
(GUID-based) filename per project.
- Updated the ReportGenerator reports glob from
`**/coverage/**/coverage.cobertura.xml` to
`**/coverage/**/*.cobertura.xml` to pick up all uniquely-named coverage
files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add TLS module ID limit to prevent unbounded growth

Implements IMPROVEMENT_FINDINGS.md section 6.2 (No Limits on Module ID Growth).

Changes:
- Add maxModuleID constant (65536) to cap the number of TLS modules
- Check module ID limit before incrementing in RegisterModule()
- Returns clear error if limit exceeded

This prevents:
- Unbounded memory growth in the DTV (Dynamic Thread Vector)
- Module ID overflow on 32-bit systems
- Memory waste with huge module IDs used as array indices

Impact: Minimal complexity increase (5.7 overall, well below threshold of 10)
Tests: All tests pass (go test ./...)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ac): explicit tool steps in skills + synthesis nudge after writes

ac-architect: numbered ÉTAPES with explicit tool calls
  1. list_files  2. code_write(INCEPTION.md)  3. output full content
  Forbid src/ writes without INCEPTION.md first

ac-codex: numbered ÉTAPES with TDD order
  1. code_read(INCEPTION.md)  2. code_write(tests)  3. code_write(impl)

executor.py: inject synthesis prompt when _written_count > 0 AND output < 80 chars
  Asks agent to show full content of written files (min 200 words)
  Compute count inline from all_tool_calls to avoid F821

commit.message:
fix(api): standardise Paddle webhook responses with formatSuccess/formatError (M-02)

Replaced 6 raw NextResponse.json() calls with formatSuccess() and
formatError() from the shared API response formatter. All webhook
responses now follow the standard { success, data/error, timestamp }
shape, consistent with every other API route.

Error responses now use ErrorHandlingService.createError() return
values directly (StructuredError) instead of discarding them and
returning ad-hoc { error: '...' } objects.

Paddle only checks HTTP status codes, so this is backward-compatible.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore ALL improvements + prevent workflow from overwriting templates

Root cause: workflow used 'git add profile/' which included templates.
When workflow ran on stale commits, it re-committed old template versions,
reverting all our fixes (CSS vars removal, skills update, footer date, etc).

Fix: workflow now only stages generated files, not templates/source files.

Restored improvements:
- No CSS custom properties in SVGs (GitHub strips them)
- Header: 'Fabio Palumbo', 'cat bio.txt', 'fpalumbo42 — zsh'
- Skills: PHP, FastAPI, Database section, ArgoCD, GCloud, Postman
- Skills: gcc -Wall -Wextra -Werror snake.c + ./a.out (split)
- Footer: 'date -r README.md' + {{DATE}} before exit (110px)
- Stats/streak: hide_border=true, border_radius=0
- Snake: BG2 for empty squares, distinct D0/D1 levels
- Snake: rect element for background color
- Snake: jsDelivr CDN + cache purge
- apply_palette.py: exports bg= for snake background
- Removed dead palette keys (A5, A6, STATS_THEME)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove 43 stale magpie-agent/ path prefixes from cross_module docs

All 6 cross_module analysis files used 'magpie-agent/' as a path prefix
in their Related Documentation sections (e.g., 'magpie-agent/modules/
module_52.md'). Since the agent's working directory IS magpie-agent/,
this prefix would resolve to 'magpie-agent/magpie-agent/modules/...'
which doesn't exist.

Removed prefix from all 43 instances across:
- carbon_balance_conservation.md (4)
- circular_dependency_resolution.md (7)
- land_balance_conservation.md (9)
- modification_safety_guide.md (11)
- nitrogen_food_balance.md (8)
- water_balance_conservation.md (4)

All 26 validation checks pass.

feat: implement v2.2 POC-scoped phases 17-19

Scope decision: This is a POC, not a production build. Removed 9
production-grade requirements from the v2.2 roadmap.

Phase 17 (POC scope) - Accessibility:
- Escape key closes detail panel, edit mode, confirmation, and
  command bar overlay (CardDetail.tsx, CommandBar.tsx)

Phase 18 (POC scope) - Operational Resilience:
- Draft edit persistence: debounced saveDraftAction output binding
  persists edited drafts to Dataverse via Canvas App handler
- Basic dismiss retry: pendingDismissals map re-fires dismiss action
  up to 3 times if card outcome doesn't change within 5 seconds

Phase 19 (POC scope) - Deployment Documentation:
- PAC CLI minimum version check (1.32+) in deploy-solution.ps1
- NuGet restore step before solution build
- Knowledge source configuration guide in deployment-guide.md
- License and role requirements matrix in deployment-guide.md
- Canvas App saveDraftAction handler in canvas-app-setup.md

Planning docs updated:
- ROADMAP.md: phases 17-19 scoped for POC, removed items documented

- STATE.md: updated to POC scope

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace UNREACHABLE with VERIFY for non-COI constraint/monic violations in nra_solver

The NRA solver's check() uses cone-of-influence (COI) to select a subset
of constraints for nlsat. When nlsat returns l_true, the model is validated
against all constraints, but non-COI constraints can legitimately be
violated since nlsat only solved over the COI subset.

- Non-COI violations gracefully return l_undef (fallback to other strategies)
- COI violations still trigger an assertion (indicating a real nlsat bug)

Fixes #8883

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: force mpv to output S32 for lossless FLAC passthrough

mpv was negotiating S16LE with PipeWire by default, silently truncating
24-bit FLAC audio to 16-bit before it reached the audio pipeline.

Add --audio-format=s32 to ensure 24-bit (and 16-bit) FLAC content is
passed losslessly as 32-bit PCM to PipeWire. Also add
--audio-samplerate=0 to let the source sample rate pass through
unmodified (no resampling).

commit.message:
Switch to manifest-based plugin discovery

Plugins now require a plugin.json manifest in their subdirectory:
  ~/.polypilot/plugins/myplugin/plugin.json

Manifest declares: entryPoint (DLL name), name, description, version.
Only directories with a valid manifest are discovered — no more listing
every transitive dependency DLL.

Settings UI shows plugin name, version, and description from manifest
instead of raw DLL filenames.

LoadEnabledProviders resolves entry-point from manifest with backward
compat for legacy direct-DLL paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: comment count corruption and follow count race condition

Bug 1 - Cache corruption zeroing out fields:
useOptimisticToggle and PostDetailScreen.handleSubmitComment called
cachePost() with only the changed fields. For posts not in the timeline
or existing cache (e.g. explore-only posts), buildCachedPostState()
defaulted non-patched fields to 0/false, corrupting comment_count,
repost_count, etc. Fix: seed cachePost with full post state via new
postToCachedState() helper so all fields are preserved.

Bug 2 - Follow count race condition:
handleFollow/Unfollow callbacks closed over a stale currentUser from
render time. Rapid follow actions would overwrite each other's count.
Fix: read latest user via useAuthStore.getState() inside the callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix/graceful unresolvable image paths (#45522)

* fix(evaluation): gracefully handle unresolvable relative image paths

When Document Intelligence indexes PDFs, it generates markdown with
references are not actual files on disk. When this markdown appears in
agent tool output via AI Search, the evaluation pipeline incorrectly
tried to resolve them as local files, causing InvalidInputError.

Modified _inline_image() in _utils.py to catch InvalidInputError from
local_to_base64() when a relative path doesn't resolve to an existing
file, and return a text dict instead of crashing. This preserves
existing behavior for valid local images, HTTP/HTTPS URLs, and data URIs.

Fixes ICM 756728740

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address Copilot review comments on inline image fallback

- Remove unused 'os' import from test module
- Replace hardcoded Path('/tmp') with pytest tmp_path fixture for portability
- Replace tempfile.TemporaryDirectory with tmp_path fixture for consistency
- Avoid exception-driven control flow: check path existence before calling
  local_to_base64() instead of catching InvalidInputError
- Lower log level from warning to debug for unresolvable image references
  to reduce noise in Document Intelligence scenarios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply black formatting to test file

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Phase 2 error handling improvements

This commit completes all Phase 2 error handling tasks from the audit:
- Symbol table load error handling (return errors instead of silencing)
- RELRO protect error handling (documented but non-fatal)
- Dynamic section validation (DT_NULL terminator required)
- PT_LOAD segment validation (Filesz <= Memsz, overflow checks)
- MemSize overflow checks (validate maxVAddr >= minVAddr)

All validation improvements follow the project's error handling patterns
and maintain backward compatibility. Tests pass with zero regressions.

Phase 1 was already complete with all critical security fixes implemented.

libqwasm.a (Qt's WASM platform plugin) uses emscripten::val internally
and webbluetooth_bridge.cpp includes emscripten/val.h. Both require the

commit.message:
Add JsonPatch.EnumerateArray API with propagator append merge (#56826)

* Add JsonPatch.EnumerateArray API with propagator append merge

Add EnumerateArray method to JsonPatch that iterates over JSON array
elements at a given path, returning each as ReadOnlyMemory<byte>.

- JsonPatch.Iterate.cs: EnumerateArray, ResolveArray, ArrayEnumerator
- JsonPatch.Implementation.cs: merge ArrayItemAppend with propagator
  results in TryGetEncodedValueInternal so GetJson stays consistent
- AvailabilitySetDataV2: test model with enhanced PropagateGet that
  serializes CLR collections via ModelReaderWriter.Write for
  no-round-trip array enumeration
- 61 tests across 7 categories covering seed-only, properties-only,
  hybrid, propagator-backed (V1 + V2), error cases, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update API listings for EnumerateArray

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant array validation byte check in EnumerateArray

The firstByte check was redundant with the ArrayEnumerator constructor
which validates via Utf8JsonReader. The byte check also couldn't handle
leading whitespace in raw JSON bytes for root-path arrays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Respect Removed state in ResolveArray root fallback

When TryGetEncodedValueInternal returns a Removed value, throw
KeyNotFoundException instead of falling back to seed bytes. This
makes EnumerateArray consistent with other JsonPatch getters.

Added tests for removed root/nested arrays and root non-array types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use computed format in exception messages instead of options.Format

Fix both read and write paths to use the resolved format variable
instead of options.Format, which could misleadingly show 'W'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changelog entry for JsonPatch.EnumerateArray

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix `_copy_output` for opaque mutable types (#1033)

* Fix _copy_output for opaque mutable types with 0 fields

Symbol, String, and similar mutable types have no Julia-visible fields
and cannot be allocated via jl_new_struct_uninit. Return them as-is when
nfields(x) == 0.

This also fixes Dict (which internally stores a Memory{Symbol}) and
any struct containing a Dict, making prepare_gradient_cache with
friendly_tangents=true work when arguments include Dict values.

commit.message:
perf: optimize feBlend with direct DataBufferInt access and integer math

Replace per-pixel getRGB()/setRGB() calls and floating-point math in the
blend filter with direct DataBufferInt raster access and integer-only
arithmetic. Also precompute blend mode as an int constant to avoid
per-pixel String switch overhead.

Benchmark (2000 iterations, 400x300 SVG with 5 blend modes):
  Before: 57.2ms avg
  After:  25.9ms avg (2.2x faster)

No regressions on other filter types (blur, drop-shadow, tile, masks).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(llm): force MiniMax tool_choice required/specific to fix tc=0

MiniMax M2.5 ignores tool_choice='auto' and never calls tools.
Fix by forcing:
- 'required' when multiple tools available (must call at least one)
- {'type':'function','function':{'name':X}} when only one tool available
  (happens after write_only_tools strip in executor)

This is the root cause of tc=0 at all rounds for MiniMax agents.
Combined with the existing tool-result visibility fix (role=tool→user),
agents can now: call tools → see results → call write tools.

commit.message:
Add import_data() and fix start() for MOTIS Docker workflow

- import_data(): generates config.yml from GTFS/OSM files, runs motis import
  via Docker. Auto-discovers .zip files, writes YAML without PyYAML dependency.
- start(): now includes --user root, --entrypoint /motis, -w /data, server
  command — matching what MOTIS actually requires.
- import_data() uses capture=False so import progress is visible in terminal.

commit.message:
fix: set EDITLESS_WORK_ITEM_URI for local tasks (#495)

* fix: set EDITLESS_WORK_ITEM_URI for local tasks

Local tasks have a filePath but no url, so the env variable was never
populated when launching a session from a local task. Now falls back to
filePath when no url is available. Also updated the custom instructions
to tell the agent to read local file paths directly.

Fix mock to return proper file:/// URI format.
Merge master to pick up v0.2 changes.

commit.message:
Add --since-last-time option to recent-scrobbles (#29)

## Summary

- Add `--since-last-time` flag to `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles` to
automatically fetch only new scrobbles since the last run
- Track scrobble count persistently in `~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
(owned 100% by spotfm)
- Every run (with or without the flag) updates the state file

## Motivation

Previously, getting new scrobbles required a manual workflow:
1. Check `data/spotify-2026.yaml` for last count
2. Check Last.FM UI for current count
3. Calculate diff manually
4. Run with `-l <diff>`

Now it's just: `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time`

## Usage

```bash
# First run — initializes state file (use -l as usual)
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 38

# Subsequent runs — fully automated
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time
# → Fetching 38 new scrobbles (was 135, now 173).
# → Artist - Track - 5 - 10 - https://...
```

## State file format

`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`:
```json
{
  "last_scrobble_count": 173,
  "last_run_date": "2026-03-06"
}
```

## Edge cases

- No state file yet → prints helpful message: "No previous state found.
Run once without --since-last-time to initialize."
- No new scrobbles → prints "No new scrobbles since last run." and
updates state

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_lastfm.py tests/test_utils.py` — 83
tests pass
- [ ] `make test` — 262 tests pass
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 5` — runs and creates
`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time` — fetches diff
automatically

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Simple Python lib to interact with Spotify and Last.FM APIs.

commit.message:
fix(blazor): fix circuit startup - use correct blazor.web.js API format

The Blazor.start() configuration was using the old blazor.server.js API format
(top-level reconnectionOptions/reconnectionHandler/dialogId), but the app uses
blazor.web.js which requires these options nested under 'circuit: { ... }'.

This caused the Blazor interactive circuit to fail to establish, resulting in:
- Tabs not responding to clicks (no @onclick handling)
- Buttons not functioning (no circuit = no event handlers)
- Accordions not expanding/collapsing (no @onclick)
- Navigation falling back to enhanced navigation (page-level)

Changes:
- Move reconnectionOptions/reconnectionHandler under circuit property
- Remove unsupported dialogId option (blazor.server.js only)
- Remove Blazor.reconnect override (blazor.server.js API, not blazor.web.js)
- Use retryIntervalMilliseconds as function for exponential backoff
- Add .catch() for circuit startup error handling
- Increment service worker cache version (v3 -> v4) to bust stale caches

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
